regions.us-ports: Today, Miami experiences clear skies with an ENE 5kt wind and less than 10% rain probability, facilitating smooth port operations and shore excursions. Heat Advisory in effect for Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 86°F) and San Diego (temperature up to 90°F). Over the next 48 hours and beyond, Miami can expect changing conditions, with overcast skies and ENE 9kt winds on Day 1, followed by ESE 16kt winds and a 20% rain probability on Day 2, and SE 11kt winds with a 25% rain probability and thunderstorms on Day 3.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, clear skies and an 11% rain probability in Nassau will facilitate smooth embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Over the next 24-48 hours, a 18% rain probability in Key West and Grand Cayman may affect shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours, a 24% rain probability in Ocho Rios is expected to impact port operations, with thunderstorms and ESE 12kt winds forecasted.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, partly cloudy conditions with an ESE 2kt wind and 16% rain probability are expected to have minimal impact on port operations in San Juan. Over the next 24-48 hours, winds will increase to E 14-15kt, potentially affecting shore excursions in St. Thomas and St. Maarten due to drizzle and a 16% rain probability on Day 1, followed by less than 10% rain probability on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, a 26% rain probability on Day 3 may affect port operations in Antigua.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, clear skies and light winds of NE 3kt are expected in Cozumel, with less than 10% rain probability. Over the next 24-48 hours, partly cloudy skies and ENE 8kt winds are forecast, with a 28% rain probability on Day 2 that may affect shore excursions in Cozumel and Costa Maya. Beyond 48 hours, thunderstorms are expected in Cozumel on Day 3, but with less than 10% rain probability.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated winds of 17kt from the east are expected in Aruba, with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability. Over the next 24-48 hours, winds will increase to 20kt in Aruba and 19kt in Curacao, with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours, winds will decrease to 17kt in Aruba, with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability, and similar conditions are expected in Bonaire and Cartagena.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, partly cloudy conditions with an ENE 11kt wind and 13% rain probability are expected in Barbados, with no significant impact on port operations. Over the next 24-48 hours, rain showers with an 18% rain probability may affect shore excursions in Barbados and nearby St. Lucia on Day 2. By Day 3, a 43% rain probability is expected to affect port operations in Barbados and potentially St. Vincent, with drizzle and E 13kt winds.
regions.eastern-pacific: Today, an Excessive Heat Warning is in effect with heat index values reaching 108F in Cabo San Lucas, posing a risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions under clear skies with NW 8kt winds. Over the next 24-48 hours, Cabo San Lucas and nearby ports like Puerto Vallarta and Mazatlan can expect the alert to continue with heat index values reaching 109F on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, elevated heat is expected to continue with a heat index reaching 103F in Cabo San Lucas on Day 3, with W 10kt winds and a 20% rain probability.
regions.los-angeles: Today, light winds of 3kt from the north and clear skies with less than 10% rain probability will provide ideal conditions for embarkation and shore excursions in Los Angeles. Over the next 24-48 hours, winds will shift to the west and west-southwest at 10-12kt, with clear to overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, affecting operations in Long Beach and Marina del Rey. Beyond 48 hours, partly cloudy skies with south winds of 7kt and less than 10% rain probability are expected, with minimal impact on cruise ship operations in the Los Angeles area, including Catalina Island.
regions.ensenada: Today, foggy conditions with a west wind at 1kt and less than 10% rain probability are expected to prevail in Ensenada. Over the next 24-48 hours, Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos can expect similar foggy conditions with west-southwest winds at 8-9kt and less than 10% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours, Ensenada may experience drizzle with a west-southwest wind at 8kt, still with less than 10% rain probability.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, an Excessive Heat condition is forecast with a heat index reaching 108F in Cabo San Lucas. Over the next 24-48 hours, San Jose del Cabo and Cabo San Lucas will experience another day of Excessive Heat with a heat index reaching 109F, followed by an Elevated Heat condition on the third day with a heat index of 103F. By the third day, a 20% rain probability may affect shore excursions in La Paz.
regions.mazatlan: Today, thunderstorms with a 40% rain probability and heat index reaching 104F may affect shore excursions in Mazatlan. Over the next 24-48 hours, Topolobampo and Altata can expect thunderstorms with a 55% rain probability on Day 2 and a 61% rain probability on Day 3, with heat index reaching 102F on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, the trend of increasing rain probability is expected to continue, potentially impacting cruise ship operations in the region.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today, a 40% rain probability and partly cloudy skies with northeast winds at 4kt may affect shore excursions in Puerto Vallarta. In the next 24-48 hours, Punta Mita and Yelapa can expect thunderstorms with a 49% rain probability and north-northeast winds at 5kt on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, Chacala is expected to see thunderstorms with a 56% rain probability and east-northeast winds at 6kt on Day 3.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, clear skies and a southeast wind of 7kt will provide favorable conditions for embarkation and shore excursions in Barcelona. Over the next 24-48 hours, partly cloudy skies with an east-southeast wind of 8kt will prevail in ports such as Valencia and Palma de Mallorca, with less than 10% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours, an increase in wind to 11kt from the east-southeast and a chance of rain showers may affect cruise ship operations in ports like Malaga and Lisbon, although the less than 10% rain probability suggests minimal impact.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, partly cloudy conditions with a less than 10% rain probability and light winds of 4kt from the SSW are expected in Naples. Over the next 24-48 hours, partly cloudy skies will give way to overcast conditions in ports like Genoa and Livorno, with winds shifting to NE 13kt on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, NW winds of 8kt and overcast skies are forecast for ports such as Sardinia and Corsica on Day 3.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, partly cloudy skies and a less than 10% rain probability will allow for smooth embarkation and disembarkation procedures in Athens. Over the next 24-48 hours, Santorini and Mykonos may experience some impacts from thunderstorms, with a 23% rain probability on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, NNW winds at 10kt and overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability are expected to prevail in the region, including Corfu and Rhodes.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today, an 80% rain probability is expected to affect shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway. Over the next 24-48 hours, rain showers with a 74% rain probability are forecast for these ports. By Day 3, conditions are expected to improve with only a 17% rain probability in Juneau.
